title: Configuring the TXT Connector
text: Configuring the TXT Connector To configure the TXT connector and data access, edit the application assignment file. The TXT connector is only available on Windows, Linux, and Unix. In the [DRIVER_SETTINGS] section, specify the connector and version. [DRIVER_SETTINGS] TXT = U2.0 Copy Optionally, define the default Unicode encoding for all entities handled by the TXT connector. Set the uni= UnicodeEncoding connector option with USYS$TXT_PARAMS . For example: [DRIVER_SETTINGS] TXT = U2.0 USYS$TXT_PARAMS uni=utf8 Copy If this is not present, the default character encoding is used, as defined by $DEF_CHARSET For more information, see uni and $def_charset . In the [PATHS] section of the assignment file, define a path for the connector: You can then use this definition in path redirections. [PATHS] $TXT TXT Copy In the [ENTITIES] section. specify the entity-to-path assignments for each entity that reads or writes text files, using the following format: Entity . Model = $txt:{ Directory /} Entity .ffd { /append } { /uni= UnicodeEncoding } /append —enables the TXT connector to read data from a text file. If omitted, the TXT connector implicitly creates the file or overwrites it. /uni —specifies the Unicode encoding to use for a specific entity. If omitted, the encoding defaults to that specified by USYS$TXT_PARAMS . For example: [ENTITIES] Entity1.cs $TXT:entity1.fdd/append/uni=utf16 Entity2.cs $TXT:entity2.fdd/append/uni=utf32 Copy Related Links Entity-to-Path Assignments Entity-to-path assignments are used to provide an additional or alternative name or path for entities.
